data.title: Top 3 things to look out for on Thursday
data.description:
As Wall Street closed mostly flat on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve decided to keep its policy rate unchanged at 3.50%-3.75%—widely expected by traders—both the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow Jones (DJI) ended flat.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), however, managed to close up +0.2%, as semiconductor companies such as Seagate Technology (STX), Intel (INTC), and Western Digital (WDC) rallied.

Seagate’s stock (STX) advanced more than 19% after the company reported stronger-than-expected results and guidance. Intel (INTC) also rallied 11% after reports that Nvidia (NVDA) may use Intel's foundry for some of their needs in 2028 for chip manufacturing.

Now, here are three things to look forward to on Thursday:

  • Thursday’s earnings: tech giant Apple (AAPL) and financial giant Visa (V) will report earnings post-market, while Mastercard Inc. (MA) and Honeywell International (HON) will publish earnings during market hours. Here is the earnings calendar.
  • On economic news, investors will await initial jobless claims for the week, as well as data on the U.S. trade deficit—which dropped to $29.4B in October, its lowest level in 16 years. Trades will also receive data on wholesale inventories and factory orders. Here is some of the latest data: jobless data rises; October wholesale inventories rise; factory orders drop.
  • Lastly, the gold spot price (XAUUSD:CUR) has reached more than $5,400, and its rally seems to continue. Gold miners (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST) could also be a leveraged play, according to Seeking Alpha Analyst Frank Holmes. “Many miners have cleaned up their balance sheets, reduced debt, and focused on shareholder returns. That means they're in a stronger position than in past gold bull markets,” he said. “I wouldn't wait for $6,000 to start participating [in the gold rally.]”

data.title: C.H. Robinson beats profit outlook, misses on revenue due to lower pricing, ocean volume slump
data.description:
Shares of C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) traded higher in after-hours trade after reporting a better-than-expected profit in the fourth quarter despite experiencing “significant market headwinds.”

"The fourth quarter certainly provided a challenging macro environment, with weak global freight demand, rising spot costs in trucking, and falling ocean rates all providing headwinds to our business," said CEO Dave Bozeman, adding that through the company’s focus on “controlling what we can control,” C.W. Robinson (CHRW) was able to consistently outperform over the last two years, and again in the fourth quarter.

Driven by market share gains, disciplined revenue management, and a “cost of hire advantage” versus the rest of the industry, the trucking and logistics company earned an adjusted profit of $1.23 per share, up 1.7% from a year earlier and 10 cents above expectations.

North American Surface Transportation ((NAST)) total volume increased ~1%, and NAST truckload volume increased ~3%, compared to a 7.6% decline in the Cass Freight Shipment Index. This resulted in a 20-basis point improvement in NAST adjusted gross profit margin to 14.6%.

While the bottom line showed modest improvement, total revenue was down 6.5% to a below consensus $3.91B, largely a result of the divestiture of its European Surface Transportation business, lower pricing and volume in its ocean division, and lower pricing in truckload services.

Cash generated by operations increased by $37.5M to $305.4M.

data.title: LendingClub stock slides after below-consensus Q1 earnings guidance
data.description:
LendingClub (LC) shares tumbled 8.3% in Wednesday after-hours trading after issuing soft earnings guidance for Q1 2026, although Q4 2025 profit and sales came in better than expected on strong originations growth.

The consumer finance company sees Q1 2026 GAAP EPS of $0.34-$0.39 (midpoint $0.365), trailing the $0.30 average analyst estimate, and Q1 loan originations of $2.55B-$2.65B (midpoint $2.60B).

For full-year 2026, EPS is expected to be $1.65-$1.80 (midpoint $1.725), and originations are targeted at $11.6B-$12.6B (midpoint $12.1B).

Q4 EPS of $0.35, topping the $0.33 Wall Street consensus, slipped from $0.37 in the prior quarter but surged from $0.08 a year ago.

Q4 net revenue of $266.5M, vs. $262.0M expected, compared with $266.2M in Q3 and $217.2M in Q4 2024.

Origination volume was $2.6B in Q4, holding steady from the earlier three-month period and +40% Y/Y.

Provision for credit losses edged up to $47.2M from $46.3M in Q3 and slid from $63.2M in Q4 2024.

Pre-provision net revenue fell to $97.2M in Q4 from $103.5M in Q3 and advanced from $74.3M in 2024's Q4.

Return on tangible common equity of 11.9% in Q4 vs. 13.2% in Q3 and 3.1% in Q4 2024.

"We're entering 2026 from a position of strength, with product innovations and marketing investments taking hold while credit continues to outperform," CEO Scott Sanborn said in a statement. "Our entry into home improvement financing is creating new opportunities and we also expect to leverage ongoing operating discipline and AI efficiencies to further strengthen the earnings power of the company."

data.title: Wheat leads U.S. crop futures higher as weak dollar, Trump biofuels support boost demand
data.description:
U.S. wheat futures surged Wednesday by the most since November, while corn and soybeans also gained, as a slide in the dollar sparked demand expectations for American crops.

A gauge of the dollar hovered near a four-year low following President Trump's comments that he was not concerned about a decline in the value of the greenback; a weaker dollar typically means that U.S. agricultural products are more competitive on the world export market.

In an Iowa speech last night, Trump promised that lawmakers were working on a plan to allow year-round nationwide sales of E15 ethanol-blended motor fuel and were "very close to getting it done."

There's "a subtle bullish shift due to recent currency action," as the Trump administration "may have decided the best way to keep U.S. physical exports competitive is a weaker dollar, which pushing the Brazilian currency strongly higher," the Hightower Report said in a note.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged as expected, but grains may react overnight and Thursday on Fed Chair Powell's comments to the press.

Wheat for March delivery (W_1:COM) closed +2.3% to $5.35 1/2 per bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade, while corn (C_1:COM) for March delivery ended +1% to $4.30 3/4 per bushel, and March soybeans (S_1:COM) settled +0.7% to $10.74 1/2 per bushel.

Shares of agriculture companies gained modestly on Wednesday, with ethanol producer Green Plains (GPRE) closing +1.3% after surging as much as 4%, and Andersons (ANDE) +0.6%, while traders Bunge (BG) and Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) gave up gains to close lower.

ETFs: (WEAT), (CORN), (SOYB), (DBA), (MOO)

data.title: What do the ratings say about Seagate Technology?
data.description:
As Seagate Technology (STX) closed up 19% on Wednesday, we look at what each type of rating says about the company.

Seagate currently sits at an interesting crossroads, judging by the three major rating sources on Seeking Alpha. The SA Quant rating is a “Hold” with a score of 3.49, Wall Street analysts sit at “Buy” with a 4.40 average, and Seeking Alpha’s own contributors also rate it “Buy” with a score of 3.62.

Together, these numbers paint a picture of a fundamentally strong but somewhat stretched cyclical stock, where institutions and independent analysts are still upbeat while the fully automated model signals caution.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system sees that the rules‑based model sees Seagate as fairly valued or mildly expensive relative to its expected growth, profitability, and recent price action, without a clear overshoot or undervaluation signal.

On Street ratings, Seagate draws a consensus “Buy” with an average score of 4.40, reflecting that most sell‑side firms still see upside ahead even after a massive rally. 

Among Seeking Alpha’s independent contributors, Seagate holds a “Buy” rating with an average score of 3.62, meaning they it above neutral but less bullishly than the pure data‑driven Quant system would.

data.title: Annaly Capital Q4 earnings beat as spreads tighten, servicing income rises
data.description:
Annaly Capital Management (NLY) turned in slightly stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings, helped by spread tightening and strong fund flows, the company said on Wednesday. Net servicing income also showed healthy growth.

Q4 EPS for distribution of $0.74 per share, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.73, rose from $0.73 in Q3 and $ in Q4 2024.

Book value per common share of $20.21 at Dec. 31, 2025, exceeding the Visible Alpha estimate of $19.65, increased from $19.25 at Sept. 30, 2025.

Economic return of 8.6% improved from 6.1% in the prior quarter. Economic leverage of 5.6x edged down from 5.7x in Q3.

"Our agency business benefited from meaningful spread tightening, supported by strong fund flows, improved fundamentals and a more favorable operating environment," said CEO and Co-Chief Investment Officer David Finkelstein. "Our residential credit platform generated record production from our whole loan correspondent channel and securitization platform, which continues to gain market share through the introduction of innovative structured transactions."

The mortgage REIT’s total assets of $135.6B at the end of Q4 climbed from $125.9B at the end of Q3 and $103.6B at the end of 2024.

Its total portfolio of $104.7B included $92.9B in highly liquid agency portfolio. Annaly’s (NLY) agency portfolio increased by 6%, representing 62% of dedicated capital, with accretive capital raised during the quarter predominantly deployed in 5.0% coupon TBA and generic collateral securities, higher coupon specified pools and agency CMBS.

Q4 net interest income of $366.6M grew from $275.8M in Q3 and $187.3M in Q4 2024. Net interest margin, excluding PAA, of % vs. 1.70% in the previous quarter and % a year ago.

Net interest spread, excluding PAA, was 1.49% compared with 1.50% in Q3 and 1.47% in the prior year’s Q4.

Net servicing income of $139.6M increased from $126.3M in the prior quarter and $115.6M a year ago.

The average economic cost of interest-bearing liabilities was 3.95% at Dec. 31, 2025, vs. 3.96% at Sept. 30, 2025, and 3.79% at Dec. 31, 2024.

Total general and administrative expenses were $51.2M vs. $50.3M in the prior quarter and $44.0M a year ago.

Conference call on Jan. 29 at 9:00 AM ET.
